source: Incus Server - 1 Install and Configuration: Installing EPEL, OpenZFS, and Incus

questions.q: Which base repository does Incus require?
questions.why: dnf install epel-release provides the required EPEL repository.

questions.q: Besides EPEL, which repository must be enabled for some special packages?
questions.why: dnf config-manager --enable crb enables the CRB repository.

questions.q: Where does the Incus package come from on Rocky 9 (before EPEL inclusion)?
questions.why: dnf copr enable neil/incus adds the COPR that provides Incus.
dnf copr enable neil/incus enables Neil Hanlon's Cool Other Package Repo.

questions.q: Which two Incus packages does the book install?
Enable and start the incus service immediately.
questions.why: --now both enables the unit at boot and starts it right away.

questions.q: The OpenZFS repository is installed from where?
questions.why: dnf install https://zfsonlinux.org/epel/zfs-release-...noarch.rpm adds the OpenZFS repo.

options: So the ZFS kernel module can be built (via DKMS) against your kernel
questions.why: dkms + kernel-devel let ZFS build its kernel module for the running kernel.
